titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD45/13/283

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=827 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:10:47.797629+00 | translation moa=826 status=live modified=2021-04-07 09:10:49.255138+00

div.p: M V H P A R G O R Y C

Cvm esset controuersia inter priorissam et conuentum monialium de Norberwic ex vna parte et abbatem et conuentum de sancto seruano ex altera . super decimis comiti de fif de omnibus firmis suis et lucris et euen
tibus et muneribus suis . et super secundis decimis ad domum suam pertinentibus de frumento farina braseo prebenda et omni genere bladi et de caseo et de butiro . Tandem procurante nobili viro malcolmo co
mite de fif vtriusque domus patrono vt perpetue pacis vtraque pars munimine roboraretur inter eos amicabilis interuenit compositio . ex vtraque parte sponte recepta . et per priorissam pro se et conuentu
suo de eorundem mandato et per abbatem pro se et conuentu suo de eorum mandato Iuramento interposito uallata . talis videlicet quod dicte priorissa et moniales singulis annis in perpetuum pro bono
pacis dictis abbati et conuentui de sancto seruano uel eorum certo nuntio litteratorie destinato viginti librarum bonorum esterlingorum persoluent apud monasterium de Norberwic medietatem ad festum sancti
Andree . et medietatem ad natiuituate sancti Iohannis baptiste . tali conditione adiecta vt si dicta pecunia ipsis uel eorum certo nuntio litteratorie destinato loco et terminis supradictis non fuerit soluta ex tunc
singulis mensibus nomine pene vna marcham argenti monacho Ianitori de sancto seruano ad ianua eiusdem monasterii per manum ipsius pauperibus distribuendam persoluant . quousque dicta pecu
nia fuerit soluta . Et pro hiis fideliter obseruandis obligauerunt bona sua mobilia et immobilia. Prefati uero abbas et conuentus propter permissas conuentiones renunciauerunt omni iuri quod habebant in permissis decimis et instrumenta que super eisdem habuerant cancellanda et in perpetuum pro irritis habenda resignauerunt . et ne contra hanc compositionem aliqua percium ullo
vnquam tempore possit reclamare vtraque pars renuntiauit omni actioni exceptioni cauillationi et impetratis et impetrandis et petitioni restitutionis in integrum et omni remedio iuris
ciuilis et canonici supponentes se iurisdictioni episcopi sancti Andree ut ipse partem resistentem ad huius compositionis obseruantiam per censuram ecclesiasticam appellatione remota comppellat
vt autem hec conuentio perpetuum robur firmitatis obtineat partes utrique parti huius scripture ad modum cyrographi confecte . Sigilla venerabilis patris . W . dei gratia episcopi sancti andree et dicti mal
colmi patroni sui simul cum sigillis suis mutuo appositis procurauerunt apponi . Hiis testibus Domino malcolmo comite de fif . Magistro petro de rameysia . Magistro . Henrico de norham . Rob’
clerico . domino . Willelmo de blar . domino nesso de ramesay . domino . Malcolmo de Manetheth . dunecano de rameysia . dunec’ de blar . Mauritio seruiente comitisse de fif . Odin’ . Malcolmo norhami .
Helya de quilta . et Multis aliis . Actum Anno gratie . Millesimo . ducentesimo . tricesimo septimo . Infra octabas apostolorum petri et pauli.
C Y R O G R A P H

Inasmuch as there was a dispute between the prioress and convent of the nuns of North Berwick on the one side, and the abbot and convent of St Serf on the other, over the teinds of the earl of Fife from all his ferms and profits and revenues and gifts, and over the second teinds belonging to the their own house from the corn, flour, hops, provender and every type of grain, cheese, and butter; at last, with the noble man, Malcolm earl of Fife, patron of both houses, acting as procurator, he interposed an amicable agreement between them, to the end that either party might be strengthened by the security of perpetual peace. Willingly received from either side, both by the prioress for herself and her convent concerning their said mandate, and by the abbot for himself and his convent for their mandate, fortified by an interposed oath, such namely that the said prioress and nuns each year in perpetuity, for the sake of peace, to the said abbot and convent of St Serf, or to their authentic representative sent by letter, shall pay twenty pounds of good sterling at the monastery of North Berwick, half at the feast of St Andrew, and half at the Nativity of St John the Baptist, with such a condition added, that if the said money were not paid to them or to their authentic representative sent by letter, at the place and times stated above, from then, each month, by way of penalty, one merk of silver is paid to the monk who is gatekeeper of St Serf at the gate of the said monastery, to be distributed by his own hand to the poor until such time as the said money has been paid. And for these things faithfully to be observed they bound their moveable and immoveable goods. The said abbot and convent, on account of the aforementioned agreements, renounced every right they used to have in the aforementioned teinds, and they unsealed the written instruments which they had held concerning these matters, to be struck through and considered invalid for ever. And lest any of the parties could at any time ever protest against this agreement, either side renounced every legal action, exception, cavil, both already brought and to be brought, and petition of restitution in full, and every remedy of civil and canon law, putting themselves under the jurisdiction of the bishop of St Andrews, so that he might compel the opposing party to the observance of this agreement by ecclesiastical censure with the appeal withdrawn. And to the end that this agreement should obtain everlasting authority and strength, the parties took care that the seals of the venerable father, William, by the grace of God bishop of St Andrews, and of the said Malcolm, their patron, were applied to each part of this written deed, drawn up in the manner of a cyrograph, with their own seal having been applied at the same time in like manner. With these witnesses, the lord Malcolm Earl of Fife, Master Peter of Ramsay, Master Henry of Norham, Rober the clerk, the lord William of Blair, the lord Ness of Ramsay, the lord Malcolm of Menteith, Duncan of Ramsay, Duncan of Blair, Maurice the sergeand of the countess of Fife, Odinel, Malcolm of Norham, Elias of Cults, and many others. Done in the year of grace 1237, within the octave of the apostles Peter and Paul.
